text.title: Thin Controller Pattern
text.summary: Controller delegating to Action classes via DI, with authorize() at the controller boundary and Resource wrapping for responses.

rule.statement: Controllers must stay under 80 lines and handle only HTTP routing, authorization, and Resource wrapping; all domain logic lives in Actions.
rule.rationale: Thin controllers prevent merge collisions when multiple agents work in parallel on the same domain. Reducing the controller to HTTP plumbing makes it stable and rarely-changed.

rule.statement: Authorize at the controller boundary; never inside an Action. Actions assume authorization already passed.
rule.rationale: Placing authorization in Actions duplicates it in every caller (HTTP, console, queue). Policy checks belong at the HTTP boundary where the actor is known.
